summary: Iteration 7 tested the two new features (Admin Dashboard + Install Banner) plus full regression. Backend 79/79 tests pass in ~71s (added TestAdmin class with 8 new tests on top of iteration_6's 71). Admin backend behavior: /auth/me returns 'role' (admin for admin@pawcare.it, 'user' otherwise); /api/admin/stats and /api/admin/users are 401 anonymous, 403 with Italian detail 'Accesso riservato agli amministratori' for non-admin, and return correct payloads for admin. /admin/users response contains user_id/email/name/auth_provider/pet_count/chat_count/has_push and no password_hash or _id. Frontend E2E on public URL (Chromium 1440x900) admin dashboard: after logging in as admin AND RELOADING, the desktop header shows an 'Admin' nav link that navigates to /admin; /admin renders 8 stat cards inside data-testid='admin-stats' (Utenti totali=43, Nuovi 7g=43, Animali=21, Notifiche attive=1, Visite=3, Vaccini=5, Antiparassitari=12, Messaggi AI=100) and data-testid='admin-users-table' with 43 admin-user-row rows including Nome/Email/Accesso(Email badge)/Registrato date/Animali/Chat/Push columns. Non-admin (freshly registered) does NOT see the Admin nav link after reload (verified no <a> pointing to /admin in the DOM), and visiting /admin directly triggers backend 403 for both /admin/stats and /admin/users (confirmed via network capture). Install Banner: on desktop Chromium the banner is hidden (beforeinstallprompt does not fire in headless — acceptable per brief). On iOS user-agent (dedicated browser context), the banner appears with data-testid='install-banner', the button labeled 'Come fare' (data-testid='install-app-button'), clicking it toggles data-testid='ios-install-help' (open/close verified), clicking data-testid='dismiss-install-banner' hides the banner AND writes localStorage.pawcare_install_dismissed='1' AND the banner stays hidden after a full page reload — dismiss persistence works. Regression: login, dashboard load with pets grid + Prossime scadenze, admin login flow, /auth/me endpoint — all working.

ui_bugs.issue: After email+password login, the Admin nav link does NOT appear immediately — only after a manual page reload. Root cause: POST /api/auth/login and POST /api/auth/register return {user:{user_id,email,name,picture}, token} WITHOUT the 'role' field; Auth.jsx calls setUser(data.user) with this partial object, so user.role is undefined and Layout.jsx's check `user?.role === 'admin'` evaluates false. Only when AuthContext.checkAuth() re-runs (page reload → GET /auth/me → includes role) does the nav link appear. Same regression will hit any admin logging in via Google OAuth session flow if that response also lacks role.

load() calls Promise.all([api.get('/admin/stats'), api.get('/admin/users')]) with no try/catch. When a non-admin visits /admin directly (which is currently allowed by the router since /admin is only Protected, not role-gated), both requests 403, throwing unhandled promise rejections and rendering the React dev error overlay in dev builds ('Uncaught runtime errors: Request failed with status code 403'). Production build won't show the overlay but the page just silently shows an empty users table with no feedback to the user. Recommend: wrap in try/catch + toast + redirect to /dashboard (or route-guard /admin behind role==='admin' in App.js).

integration_issues.flow: Admin login → immediate admin nav visibility
integration_issues.issue: The login response missing 'role' means the admin has to reload before they can see the Admin link. This directly contradicts the review request which states: 'When logged in as admin, the nav shows an Admin link'.

action_items: FIX auth response shape: include 'role' in the user object returned by POST /api/auth/login, POST /api/auth/register, AND POST /api/auth/google/session so that setUser(data.user) in Auth.jsx / AuthCallback.jsx populates role immediately. Alternative: change Auth.jsx/AuthCallback.jsx to call checkAuth() instead of setUser(data.user) after successful login so a fresh /auth/me hits.

Guard the /admin route: either add a role-based redirect in App.js (e.g. <Route path='/admin' element={<Protected role='admin'><Admin/></Protected>} />) OR wrap Admin.jsx load() in try/catch and show a friendly 'Accesso riservato' state when the API returns 403.

(Optional) Add data-testid="nav-admin" to the mobile bottom-nav link as well; it currently only exists on the desktop header link (the mobile version reuses `nav.map` without the data-testid attribute injected).
critical_code_review_comments: Auth.jsx line 37: setUser(data.user) loses the role field because the /auth/login response omits it. Either backend must include role or the frontend must refetch /auth/me. Same for AuthCallback.jsx after Google OAuth.
Admin.jsx load(): no error handling — API errors bubble as unhandled promise rejections. Add try/catch + user-facing state (403 → 'Accesso riservato'; 5xx → 'Errore di caricamento').

App.js /admin route is not role-guarded — any authenticated user can navigate there and just see an empty (but potentially error-overlay-triggering) view. Add role gating in the router for defense in depth.
Layout.jsx mobile bottom nav map (line 118) does not apply the data-testid='nav-...' attribute (only the desktop header map on line 74 does), making mobile nav slightly less testable.
InstallBanner.jsx: solid implementation; correctly guards against standalone display-mode and dismiss localStorage; iOS/Android branching + cleanup on unmount all look good.
Admin.jsx: no pagination on users table — with 43 users the table is short, but at scale (>500) this will scroll heavily. Consider virtualization or server-side pagination later.
server.py get_current_user is now called twice for every admin request (once by Depends, then _require_admin runs) — negligible perf hit, but the pattern is fine.
Carryover from prior iterations (unresolved): server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event handlers and sync `requests` inside async endpoints.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added TestAdmin class with 8 new tests — total 79)

success_rate.frontend: 80% — admin dashboard rendering + non-admin gating + install banner all work correctly EXCEPT the setUser-loses-role bug that hides the Admin nav until reload

test_credentials: admin@pawcare.it / admin123 (verified working, returns role=admin from /auth/me). Ephemeral non-admin: test_gate_<ts>@example.com / Pass1234!
seed_data_creation: TestAdmin uses the fixtures from earlier classes (admin_session, new_user_session) — no new seed data. Non-admin gate UI test creates a throwaway test_gate_* account via /auth/register.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend admin endpoints work correctly and are fully covered by TestAdmin (8/8) in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Frontend BUG confirmed: after admin login the desktop 'Admin' nav appears ONLY after a full page reload — because Auth.jsx#submit does `setUser(data.user)` with the /auth/login response which lacks 'role'. Two clean fixes: (a) backend include role in login/register/google-session responses, or (b) frontend Auth.jsx calls await checkAuth() after login instead of setUser(data.user). Also recommend guarding /admin in App.js and wrapping Admin.jsx#load in try/catch to avoid the dev-overlay 403 crash for non-admins hitting /admin directly. iOS install banner path is fully verified (Come fare button → ios-install-help toggle → dismiss + localStorage persistence + reload). Android/Chrome beforeinstallprompt does not fire in headless — banner correctly hidden, not a bug.
rca of the issue: Auth.jsx line 37 destructures the /auth/login response and stores response.user in AuthContext. /auth/login returns {user:{user_id, email, name, picture}, token} — no role. Layout.jsx line 26 gates the Admin nav on `user?.role === 'admin'` which is false because role is undefined. On refresh, AuthContext.checkAuth() runs in useEffect → GET /auth/me which DOES return role (server.py line 300) → user object updated → nav re-renders with the Admin link. Reproduction: 1) POST /auth/login with admin creds; 2) inspect the returned JSON — no `role`; 3) frontend setUser(data.user) → user.role undefined; 4) Admin nav hidden. Mitigation options (any one): (a) server.py login()/register()/google_session() include role in the returned user dict (single-line change); (b) Auth.jsx#submit await checkAuth() instead of setUser(data.user); (c) AuthCallback.jsx does the same after Google session exchange.
